topic: Claude Code /ultrareview Command: What It Does and When to Use It

key_points: Official Anthropic docs: /ultrareview launches a fleet of cloud-hosted reviewer agents
Two modes: no args reviews local branch diff vs default branch; /ultrareview <PR#> reviews a GitHub PR
Requires Claude Code v2.1.86+ and a Claude account login (API key alone insufficient)
Background task — you can keep working during the 5–10 minute review window
Staged and uncommitted changes included in no-arg mode
Each subagent reviews through a different lens: logic, security, performance, error handling, test coverage
Find-then-Verify two-stage pipeline keeps false positive rate under 1%
In-house Anthropic engineers marked fewer than 1% of findings as incorrect
On PRs over 1,000 lines, 84% of reviews return findings averaging 7.5 issues each
Reserve for commits where a production bug would cost more than $20 to fix
$5–$20 per run billed to extra usage after 3 free runs (Pro and Max)
3 free runs are a one-time allotment per account; expired May 5, 2026
Larger diffs cost more — small PRs under 50 lines cost less, large multi-file PRs at the high end
Not a replacement for human judgment on business logic and architecture
Confirmation dialog shown before launch so you can cancel without being billed
Real-world test on 11,000-line voice calling PR: 64 candidate bugs found, then verified down to confirmed set, 17 min total
Reviewer found bugs it then proved were real before reporting them — no false positives in that run
A one-line auth change at Anthropic that would have silently broken login flagged as critical
TrueNAS ZFS encryption refactor: surfaced type mismatch wiping encryption key cache on every sync
Quality beats /review for complex changes; overkill for trivial diffs
Covers: correctness (logic errors, edge cases, off-by-ones, null handling, race conditions)
Covers: security (injection vectors, auth/authz gaps, secret handling)
Covers: architecture (pattern violations, dependency direction), tests, performance (N+1, loops), style
Runs as cloud agent fleet — no local compute consumed
Findings merged into a single structured report delivered back to your Claude Code session

stats.fact: Anthropic engineers marked fewer than 1% of ultrareview findings as incorrect in internal testing
On PRs over 1,000 lines, 84% of ultrareview runs return findings averaging 7.5 confirmed issues each
Typical review takes 5–10 minutes and costs $5–$20 per run depending on diff size
A real test on an 11,000-line PR produced 64 candidate bugs in the Find stage and took 17 minutes total
Pro and Max subscribers received 3 free ultrareview runs (one-time allotment, expired May 5, 2026)
Requires Claude Code v2.1.86 or later and a Claude account login (not just an API key)

angles: The two-stage Find → Verify pipeline is the key differentiator that keeps false positives under 1% — explain that architecture
Cost-benefit framing: when does a $5–$20 review pay for itself vs. /review or no automated review?
No-arg local-branch mode (no GitHub remote needed) is undersold — great for teams without public repos
Compare ultrareview vs. /review vs. /security-review — each has a distinct niche
Real-world war stories: auth bypass, encryption key wipe — concrete examples build trust
Known limitations: won't catch business-logic errors only a domain expert understands; still needs human review for architecture
Workflow integration tips: when in the PR lifecycle to run it (pre-push vs. draft PR vs. ready-for-review)
keywords: Claude Code bug hunting before merge
Claude Code ultrareview how to use
